info.name: MuleSoft-to-SpringBoot Migrator — Complete API Collection
info.description:
Comprehensive Postman collection covering ALL API endpoints for the MuleSoft-to-Spring Boot Agentic AI Migration Platform.

Version: 2.0
Date: April 2026
Base URL: {{base_url}}/api/v2

Setup:
1. Set 'base_url' variable to your Azure Function App URL
2. Set 'auth_token' after calling Login or use Azure AD token
3. Set 'admin_email' and 'admin_password' for admin endpoints

request.description:
Liveness probe. Returns service health status including database, Redis, RAG, and LLM connectivity.

Expected: 200 OK
No authentication required.

Returns the currently authenticated user's profile.

Expected: 200 OK with { email, display_name, role, azure_ad_oid }

item.name: 02 — Migrations
item.description: Core migration CRUD and workflow endpoints

body.raw:
{
    "project_name": "customer-service",
    "source_type": "mule4",
    "target_framework": "spring-boot",
    "group_id": "com.example",
    "artifact_id": "customer-service",
    "base_package": "com.example.customerservice",
    "java_version": "21",
    "spring_boot_version": "3.2",
    "build_tool": "maven",
    "include_tests": true,
    "include_swagger": true,
    "include_docker": true,
    "xml_files": [
        {
            "name": "customer-api.xml",
            "content": "<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<mule xmlns='http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core'
      xmlns:http='http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http'
      xmlns:db='http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/db'
      xmlns:ee='http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core'>

    <http:listener-config name='HTTP_Listener_config'>
        <http:listener-connection host='0.0.0.0' port='8081'/>
    </http:listener-config>

    <flow name='getCustomersFlow'>
        <http:listener config-ref='HTTP_Listener_config' path='/api/customers' method='GET'/>
        <logger level='INFO' message='Fetching all customers'/>
        <db:select config-ref='Database_Config'>
            <db:sql>SELECT * FROM customers</db:sql>
        </db:select>
        <ee:transform>
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
        </ee:transform>
    </flow>
</mule>"
        }
    ],
    "llm_config": {
        "enabled": true,
        "provider": "github-copilot",
        "model": "gpt-4o"
    }
}

request.description:
Create a new migration job by pasting MuleSoft XML directly.

The migration is enqueued for async processing. Poll GET /migrations/{id} for status.

Expected: 202 Accepted with migration object (status: 'queued')

The test script saves the migration_id for subsequent requests.

Upload a MuleSoft project as a ZIP file.

Supports:
- Single XML files
- ZIP archives with multiple XMLs
- Full MuleSoft project folders

Automatically detects: XML files, pom.xml, DataWeave scripts (.dwl), RAML specs, config files.

Expected: 202 Accepted

List all migrations for the current user with pagination.

Query Parameters:
- page (default: 1)
- page_size (default: 20, max: 100)
- status (optional filter)

Expected: 200 OK with { items: [...], total, page, page_size }

Get full details of a migration including status, output files, agent traces, and summary.

Poll this endpoint every 3s while status is 'queued' or 'running'.

Expected: 200 OK with full migration object

Download a single generated file by its path.

Expected: 200 OK with file content (text/plain)

Update specific files in a completed migration (e.g., after manual edits in the UI).

Expected: 200 OK

Cancel a running or queued migration.

Expected: 200 OK with updated migration (status: 'cancelled')

Retry a failed or stuck migration. Resets status to 'queued' and re-enqueues.

Expected: 200 OK with updated migration

Soft-delete a migration (sets deleted_at timestamp).

Expected: 200 OK

Get aggregate migration statistics.

Returns: total, successful, failed, avg_duration_ms, total_tokens, success_rate, weekly_counts, monthly_counts

Expected: 200 OK

Get AI agent execution traces for a migration.

Returns: Array of agent traces with status, tokens, duration, RAG context used, errors.

Expected: 200 OK

Trigger a Maven build for a completed migration.

The build runs via ACR Quick Build (Maven + Docker).
Poll GET /builds/{id} for status and logs.

Expected: 202 Accepted with build object (status: 'queued')

Get build status and logs.

Poll every 3s while status is 'running'.

Returns: { id, migration_id, status, exit_code, build_log, duration_ms, log_lines: [...] }

Expected: 200 OK

List all builds for a specific migration.

Expected: 200 OK with { items: [...] }

Cancel a running build.

Expected: 200 OK

item.name: 04 — Validations
item.description: Deploy, test, and validate generated Spring Boot apps

request.description:
Create a validation job: builds Docker image, deploys to ACI, waits for health check.

Workflow:
1. Build Docker image via ACR
2. Deploy to Azure Container Instances
3. Wait for /actuator/health (up to 180s)
4. Status transitions: pending → building_image → deploying → running

Poll GET /validations/{id} for status.

Expected: 202 Accepted

Get validation status including ACI details.

Poll every 3-5s while status is 'pending', 'building_image', or 'deploying'.

Returns: { id, status, aci_name, aci_fqdn, app_url, test_results, user_verdict, expires_at }

Expected: 200 OK

List all validation jobs for a specific migration.

Expected: 200 OK with { items: [...] }

Run side-by-side API comparison.

Calls each test_endpoint on BOTH the MuleSoft app and the deployed Spring Boot app.
Compares status codes and response bodies.

Returns: { results: [ { method, path, match, mulesoft: {status, body}, springboot: {status, body} } ] }

Expected: 200 OK

Submit manual test verdict.

Options: 'pass', 'fail', 'partial'

Expected: 200 OK with updated validation

Manually teardown the ACI container (stop running app).

Note: Containers auto-teardown after keep_alive_min expires.

Expected: 200 OK

item.name: 05 — RAG Knowledge Base
item.description: Semantic search and knowledge base management

request.description:
Search the RAG knowledge base using semantic similarity.

Uses text-embedding-3-large (3072 dims) + pgvector cosine similarity.

Returns: { results: [ { title, content, category, score, metadata } ] }

Expected: 200 OK

Index the default migration pattern knowledge base.

Requires admin privileges.
Seeds 100+ migration patterns covering HTTP, DB, JMS, etc.

Expected: 200 OK with { indexed_count: N }

Add a new document to the RAG knowledge base.

The document is embedded using text-embedding-3-large and stored in pgvector.

Expected: 201 Created

item.name: 06 — GitHub Integration
item.description: Push generated code to GitHub repositories

body.raw:
{
    "migration_id": "{{migration_id}}",
    "repo_name": "your-org/customer-service-springboot",
    "branch": "springboot-migration",
    "commit_message": "Automated migration from MuleSoft to Spring Boot",
    "github_pat": "ghp_your_github_personal_access_token"
}

request.description:
Push all generated files to a GitHub repository.

Requires a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) with 'repo' scope.
PAT can be provided in request body or configured in Azure Key Vault.

Returns: { commit_sha, commit_url, files_pushed }

Expected: 200 OK

List accessible GitHub repositories for the configured token.

Expected: 200 OK with repo list

Check GitHub integration status (whether PAT is configured and valid).

Expected: 200 OK with { connected, username, avatar_url }

TROUBLESHOOTING: Migration stuck in 'queued' status

Steps:
1. Check this endpoint — verify status is still 'queued'
2. Check health endpoint — verify queue connectivity
3. If queue is healthy, try POST /migrations/{id}/retry
4. Check Application Insights for queue trigger errors
5. Verify Azure Storage Queue is not paused

TROUBLESHOOTING: Migration failed

Steps:
1. Check this endpoint — look at 'error' field
2. Common errors:
   - 'XXE validation failed' → XML contains external entities
   - 'No flows found in XML' → Invalid MuleSoft XML structure
   - 'LLM provider error' → API key expired or provider down
3. Check 'agent_trace' for detailed AI decision log
4. Try POST /migrations/{id}/retry after fixing the issue

TROUBLESHOOTING: Build failed

Steps:
1. Check build_log for Maven errors
2. Common build failures:
   - 'package does not exist' → Missing import in generated Java
   - 'cannot find symbol' → Missing class reference
   - 'Compilation failure' → Syntax error in generated code
   - 'Cannot load driver class' → Database driver mismatch
3. Fix: Edit migration files, then create a new build
4. The validation pipeline auto-patches many common issues

TROUBLESHOOTING: Validation deployment failed

Steps:
1. Check validation status and error field
2. Check container logs: GET /validations/{id}/logs
3. Common failures:
   - 'Health check timeout' → App didn't start in 180s
   - 'Image build failed' → Same as build failures above
   - 'ACI quota exceeded' → Too many containers running
4. Check ACI logs for Spring Boot startup errors
5. Teardown failed containers: POST /validations/{id}/teardown

TROUBLESHOOTING: API comparison shows mismatches

Steps:
1. Run comparison and examine results
2. For each mismatch, check:
   - Status code differences → Controller mapping or error handling issue
   - Response body differences → DataWeave conversion issue
   - Missing fields → Incomplete mapping in flow converter
3. Edit the generated controller/service code
4. Re-build and re-validate
5. Or submit verdict as 'partial' and document differences

TROUBLESHOOTING: Getting 401 Unauthorized

Steps:
1. Re-login to get a fresh token
2. Token is automatically saved to auth_token variable
3. If using Azure AD, get a new access token from MSAL
4. Check if ADMIN_EMAIL matches in Azure Function App Settings

item.name: T7: RAG Search Returns No Results

request.description:
TROUBLESHOOTING: RAG search returns empty results

Steps:
1. Try this search with very low min_score (0.1)
2. If still empty: Knowledge base may not be seeded
3. Run POST /rag/seed to index the default patterns
4. Check GET /rag/collections — should show document count > 0
5. Verify PostgreSQL has pgvector extension enabled
6. Check Azure OpenAI endpoint for embedding errors
